NI-DCPower switches the power source used by the device to the specified value.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE_INUse</td><td>Indicates whether the device is using the internal or auxiliary power source to generate power.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR SELF CALIBRATION PERSISTENCE</td><td>Specifies whether the values calculated during self-calibration should be written to hardware to be used until the next self-calibration or only used until the niDCPower ResetDevice function is called or the machine is powered down.</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUXILIARY_POWER_SOURCE_AWER_SOURCE_AVAILABLE

Indicates whether an auxiliary power source is connected to the device.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUXILIARY_POWER_SOURCE_AVAILABLE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150002</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported in sessions initialized with independent

channels.

A value of VI_FALSE may indicate that the auxiliary input fuse has blown. Refer to theDetecting Internal/Auxiliary Power topic in the NI DC Power Supplies and SMUs Help for more information about internal and auxiliary power.




# Note

This attribute does not necessarily indicate if the device is using the auxiliarypower source to generate power. Use theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE_IN_USE attribute to retrieve thisinformation.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_INTERLOCK_INPUT_OPEN

Indicates whether the safety interlock input is open.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_INTERLOCK_INPUT_OPEN

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150105</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>Instruments</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Safety interlock input is closed.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true (1)</td><td>Safety interlock input is open.</td></tr></table>




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify an instrument name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_ISOL TTR_ISOLATION_STATE

Specifies whether the channel is isolated.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_ISOLATION_STATE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150302</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ISOLATED</td><td>1128(0x468)</td><td>The channel is disconnected from chassis ground.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NON_ISOLATED</td><td>1129(0x469)</td><td>The channel is connected to chassis ground.</td></tr></table>

Default Value: Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE

For sessions initialized with deprecated initialize functions, this attribute specifies thepower source to use. NI-DCPower switches the power source used by the device to thespecified value.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150000</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

This attribute is not supported in sessions initialized with theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_INTERNAL</td><td>1003(0x3eb)</td><td>Uses the PXI chassis power source.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALAuxILIARY</td><td>1004(0x3ec)</td><td>Uses the auxiliary power source connected to the device.Only the NI 4110 and NI 4130 support this value.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATIC</td><td>1005(0x3ed)</td><td>Uses the auxiliary power source if it is available; otherwise uses the PXI chassis power source.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATIC




# Note

Automatic selection is not persistent and occurs only at the time this

attribute is set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATIC. However, if the session is inthe Committed or Uncommitted state when you set this attribute, the powersource selection only occurs after you call theniDCPower_InitiateWithChannels function for all channels in the session.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE_IN_USE

Indicates whether the device is using the internal or auxiliary power source to generatepower.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_SOURCE_IN_USE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150001</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported in sessions initialized with independentchannels.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_INTERNAL</td><td>1003(0x3eb)</td><td>Uses the PXI chassis power source.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUXILIARY</td><td>1004(0x3ec)</td><td>Uses the auxiliary power source connected to the device.Only the NI PXI-4110, NI PXIe-4112, NI PXIe-4113, and NI PXI-4130 support this value. This is the only supported value for the NI PXIe-4112 and NI PXIe-4113.</td></tr></table>

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SELF_CALIBRATTR_SELF_CALIBRATION_PERTION_PERSISTENCE

Specifies whether the values calculated during self-calibration should be written tohardware to be used until the next self-calibration or only used until theniDCPower_ResetDevice function is called or the machine is powered down.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SELF_CALIBRATION_PERSISTENCE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150073</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Instruments</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

This attribute affects the behavior of the niDCPower_CalSelfCalibrate function. Whenset to NIDCPOWER_VAL_KEEP_IN_MEMORY, the values calculated by theniDCPower_CalSelfCalibrate function are used in the existing session, as well as in allfurther sessions until you call the niDCPower_ResetDevice function or restart the

machine. When you set this property to NIDCPOWER_VAL_WRITE_TO_EEPROM, thevalues calculated by the niDCPower_CalSelfCalibrate function are written to hardwareand used in the existing session and in all subsequent sessions until another call to theniDCPower_CalSelfCalibrate function is made.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify an instrument name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_KEEP_INMEMORY</td><td>1045(0x415)</td><td>Keep new self-calibration values in memory only.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_WRITE_TO_EEEPROM</td><td>1046(0x416)</td><td>Write new self-calibration values to hardware.Refer to your device documentation for more information about the implications of frequent writes to the EEPROM.</td></tr></table>

Default Value: NIDCPOWER_VAL_KEEP_IN_MEMORY

LCR

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CABLE_LENGTH</td><td>Specifies how to apply cable compensation data for instruments that support LCR functionality.</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CABLE_LENGTH

Specifies how to apply cable compensation data for instruments that support LCRfunctionality.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CABLE_LENGTH

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150278</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Supported instruments use cable compensation for the following operations:

• SMU mode: to stabilize DC current sourcing in the two smallest current ranges

• LCR mode: to compensate for the effects of cabling on LCR measurements




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

For NI standard options, select the length of your NI cable to apply compensation datafor a typical cable of that type.

For custom options, choose the source of the custom cable compensation data. Youmust then generate the custom cable compensation data.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ZERO_M</td><td>1121(0x461)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for a 0m cable (direct connection).</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_0_5M</td><td>1153(0x481)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 0.5m coaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_1M</td><td>1122(0x462)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 1m coaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_2M</td><td>1123(0x463)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 2m coaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_4M</td><td>1124(0x464)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 4m coaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_TRIAXIAL_1M</td><td>1139(0x473)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 1m triaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_TRIAXIAL_2M</td><td>1140(0x474)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 2m triaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_NI_STANDARD_TRIAXIAL_4M</td><td>1141(0x475)</td><td>Uses predefined cable compensation data for an NI standard 4m triaxial cable.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL/custom_ONBOARD_STORAGE</td><td>1125(0x465)</td><td>Uses previously generated custom cable compensation data from onboard</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>storage. Only the most recently performed compensation data for each custom cable compensation type (open, short) is stored.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL/custom_AS_CONFIGURED</td><td>1126(0x466)</td><td>Uses the custom cable compensation data supplied to niDCPower_ConfigureLCRCompensation.Use this option to manage multiple sets of custom cable compensation data.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT

Specifies the behavior of the Measure Complete event.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_PULSE_POLARITY

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150044</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALACTIVE_HIGH</td><td>1018(0x3fa)</td><td>A high pulse occurs when the event is generated. The exported signal is low level both before and after the event is generated.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALACTIVE_LOW</td><td>1019(0x3fb)</td><td>A low pulse occurs when the event is generated. The exported signal is high level both before and after the event is generated.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_ACTIVE_HIGH

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT

Specifies the width of the Measure Complete event, in seconds.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_PULSE_WIDTH

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150045</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The minimum event pulse width value for PXI devices is 150 ns, and the minimumevent pulse width value for PXI Express devices is 250 ns.

The maximum event pulse width value for all devices is 1.6 microseconds.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

Valid Values: 1.5e-7 to 1.6e-6

Default Value: The default value for PXI Express devices is 250 ns.

Toggle

# Groups

None

# Group members

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER ATTR MEASURE COMPLETE EVENT TOGGLE INITIAL STATE</td><td>Specifies the initial state of the metric NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_STATUS to NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUT</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT

Specifies the initial state of the measure complete event if you set theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR toNIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_TOGGLE.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150334</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

For more information about supported devices and terminals, searchSupported Attributes by Device on ni.com.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_LOW</td><td>1149(0x47d)</td><td>The output is set to low at session commit. For operation in Single Point source mode, the output switches to high when the event occurs during the acquisition. The output then switches to low if a subsequent event occurs during the acquisition. This pattern repeats as events continue to be generated. For operation in Sequence source mode, the output switches to high the first time an event occurs during the acquisition. It then switches to low the</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>second time an event occurs. This pattern repeats for any subsequent event occurrences.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_HIGH</td><td>1150(0x47e)</td><td>The output is set to high at session commit. For operation in Single Point source mode, the output switches to low when the event occurs during the acquisition. The output then switches to high if a subsequent event occurs during the acquisition. This pattern repeats as events continue to be generated. For operation in Sequence source mode, the output switches to low the first time an event occurs during the acquisition. It then switches to high the second time an event occurs. This pattern repeats for any subsequent event occurrences.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_LOW

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_CTTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_DELOMPLETE_EVENT_DELAY

Specifies the amount of time to delay the generation of the Measure Complete event,in seconds.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_DELAY

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150046</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Valid Values: The PXIe-4051 supports values from 0 seconds to 39 seconds. ThePXIe-4147 supports values from 0 seconds to 26.5 seconds. The PXIe-4150/4151supports values from 0 seconds to 42 seconds. The PXIe-4162/4163 support valuesfrom 0 seconds to 23 seconds. All other supported instruments support values from 0to 167 seconds.

Default Value: Varies by device. Refer to the Supported Attributes by Device topic inthe NI DC Power Supplies and SMUs Help for more information about defaultvalues.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR

Determines how the event type behaves when the corresponding trigger is received.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150333</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

For more information about supported devices and terminals, searchSupported Attributes by Device on ni.com.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_PULSE</td><td>1147 (0x47b)</td><td>A single 250ns pulse is transmitted.</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_TOGGLE</td><td>1148(0x47c)</td><td>The output level toggles between low and high. The initial output level is determined by the "toggle initial state" attribute for the event.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_PULSE

The "toggle initial state" attribute used to set the initial output level for

NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_TOGGLE is

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

Specifies the output terminal for exporting the Measure Complete event.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150047</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Specify the output terminal using the form /Dev1/PXI_Trig0, where Dev1 is theinstrument and PXI_Trig0 is the terminal.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READ TTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGERSE_TRIGGER

Specifies the behavior of the Ready For Pulse Trigger event.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGER_EVENT_PULSE_POLARITY

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150103</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALACTIVE_HIGH</td><td>1018(0x3fa)</td><td>A high pulse occurs when the event is generated. The exported signal is low level both before and after the event is generated.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALACTIVE_LOW</td><td>1019(0x3fb)</td><td>A low pulse occurs when the event is generated. The exported signal is high level both before and after the event is generated.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_ACTIVE_HIGH

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READ TTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGERSE_TRIGGER

Specifies the width of the Ready For Pulse Trigger event, in seconds.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGER_EVENT_PULSE_WIDTH

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>1150104</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The minimum event pulse width value for PXI Express devices is 250 ns.

The maximum event pulse width value for all devices is 1.6 microseconds.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Default Value: The default value for PXI Express devices is 250 ns.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READTTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGER_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINALSE_TRIGGER_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

Specifies the output terminal for exporting the Ready For Pulse Trigger event.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_READY_FOR_PULSE_TRIGGER_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150102</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Specify the output terminal using the form /Dev1/PXI_Trig0, where Dev1 is theinstrument and PXI_Trig0 is the terminal.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

• niDCPower_ExportSignalWithChannels

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SEQUENCE_ENGINE_DONE_E

Specifies the initial state of the sequence engine done event if you set theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_SEQUENCE_ENGINE_DONE_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR toNIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_TOGGLE.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SEQUENCE_ENGINE_DONE_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150346</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

For more information about supported devices and terminals, searchSupported Attributes by Device on ni.com.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_LOW</td><td>1149(0x47d)</td><td>The output is set to low at session commit. For operation in Single Point source mode, the output switches to high when the event occurs during the acquisition. The output then switches to low if a subsequent event occurs during the acquisition. This pattern repeats as events continue to be generated. For operation in Sequence source mode, the output switches to high the first time an event occurs during the acquisition. It then switches to low the second time an event occurs. This pattern repeats for any subsequent event occurrences.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_HIGH</td><td>1150(0x47e)</td><td>The output is set to high at session commit. For operation in Single Point source mode, the output switches to low when the event occurs during the acquisition. The output then switches to high if a subsequent event occurs during the acquisition. This pattern repeats as events continue to be generated. For operation in Sequence source mode, the output switches to low the first time an event occurs during the acquisition. It then switches to high the second time an event occurs. This pattern repeats for any subsequent event occurrences.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE_LOW

The initial output level is determined by the "toggle initial</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>state" attribute for the event.</td></tr></table>

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_PULSE</td><td>1147(0x47b)</td><td>A single 250ns pulse is transmitted.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_TOGGLE</td><td>1148(0x47c)</td><td>The output level toggles between low and high. The initial output level is determined by the &quot;toggle initial state&quot; attribute for the event.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_PULSE

The "toggle initial state" attribute used to set the initial output level forNIDCPOWER_VAL_EVENT_OUTPUT_BEHAVIOR_TOGGLE isNIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_COMPLETE_EVENT_TOGGLE_INITIAL_STATE.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

Specifies the output terminal for exporting the Source Complete event.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_COMPLETE_EVENT_OUTPUT_TERMINAL

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150043</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Specify the output terminal using the form /Dev1/PXI_Trig0, where Dev1 is theinstrument and PXI_Trig0 is the terminal.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_ExportSignalWithChannels

Advanced Session Information

# Groups

None

# Group members

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR.Driver_setup</td><td>Indicates the Driver Setup string that you specified when initializing the driver.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_IO_RESOURCE Descriptor</td><td>Indicates the resource descriptor NI-DCPower uses to identify the resource(s) used by the NI-DCPower session.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LOGICAL_NAME</td><td>Contains the logical name(s) you specified when opening the current IVI session.</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_DRIVER_SETUP

Indicates the Driver Setup string that you specified when initializing the driver.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_DRIVER_SETUP

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050007</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Some cases exist where you must specify the instrument driver options at initializationtime. An example of this case is specifying a particular device model from among afamily of devices that the driver supports. This attribute is useful when simulating adevice. You can specify the driver-specific options through the DriverSetup keyword inthe optionsString parameter in the niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannelsfunction or through the IVI Configuration Utility.

If you do not specify a Driver Setup string, this attribute returns an empty string.

# High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_IO_RESOURCE_DESCRIPTOR

Indicates the resource descriptor NI-DCPower uses to identify the resource(s) used bythe NI-DCPower session.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_IO_RESOURCE_DESCRIPTOR

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050304</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The resource name specified with the niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannelsfunction can include instrument(s) and/or channel(s) but does not support logicalnames.

If you initialize NI-DCPower with a logical name using a deprecated initialize VI, thisattribute contains the resource descriptor that corresponds to the entry in the IVIConfiguration utility.

If you initialize NI-DCPower with the resource descriptor, this attribute contains thatvalue.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LOGICAL_NAME

Contains the logical name(s) you specified when opening the current IVI session.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LOGICAL_NAME

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>1050305</td><td>ViString</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

IVI logical names are not supported in sessions created using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

You can pass a logical name to a deprecated initialize function. The IVI Configurationutility must contain an entry for the logical name. The logical name entry refers to afunction section in the IVI Configuration file. The function section specifies a physicalinstrument and initial user options.

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CACHE</td><td>Specifies whether to cache the value of attributes.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_INTERCHANGE_CHECK</td><td>Specifies whether to perform interchangeability checking and log interchangeability warnings when you call NI-DCPower functions. VI_true specifies that interchangeability checking is enabled.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_QUERY_INSTRUMENT_STATUS</td><td>Specifies whether NI-DCPower queries the instrument status after each operation.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RANGE_CHECK</td><td>Specifies whether to validate attribute values and function parameters.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_record_COERCIONS</td><td>Specifies whether the IVI engine records the value coercions it makes for ViInt32 and ViReal64 attributes. Call the niDCPower_GetNextCoercionRecord function to read and delete the earliest coercion record from the list.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SIMULATE</td><td>Specifies whether to simulate NI-DCPower I/O operations. VI_true specifies that operation is simulated.</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CACHE

Specifies whether to cache the value of attributes.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CACHE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050004</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

When caching is enabled, NI-DCPower records the current instrument settings andavoids sending redundant commands to the instrument. Enabling caching cansignificantly increase execution speed.

NI-DCPower might always cache or never cache particular attributes regardless of thesetting of this attribute.

Use the niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function to override thisvalue.

**Default Value:**VI_TRUE

# High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_InitializeWithChannels

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_INTERCHANGE_CHECK

Specifies whether to perform interchangeability checking and log interchangeabilitywarnings when you call NI-DCPower functions. VI_TRUE specifies thatinterchangeability checking is enabled.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_INTERCHANGE_CHECK

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050021</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Interchangeability warnings indicate that using your application with a differentinstrument might cause different behavior. Call theniDCPower_GetNextInterchangeWarning function to retrieve interchange warnings.

Call the niDCPower_GetNextInterchangeWarning function to clear the list ofinterchangeability warnings without reading them.

Interchangeability checking examines the attributes in a capability group only if youspecify a value for at least one attribute within that group. Interchangeability warningscan occur when an attribute affects the behavior of the instrument and you have notset that attribute or when the attribute has been invalidated since you set it.

Default Value: VI_FALSE

High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_QUER TTR_QUERY_INSTRUMENT_S TRUMENT_STATUS

Specifies whether NI-DCPower queries the instrument status after each operation.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_QUERY_INSTRUMENT_STATUS

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050003</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Querying the instrument status is useful for debugging. After you validate your

program, you can set this attribute to VI_FALSE to disable status checking andmaximize performance.

NI-DCPower ignores status checking for particular attributes regardless of the settingof this attribute.

Use the niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function to override thisvalue.

Default Value: VI_TRUE

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RANGE_CHECK

Specifies whether to validate attribute values and function parameters.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RANGE_CHECK

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050002</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

If this attribute is enabled, NI-DCPower validates the parameter values that you pass toNI-DCPower functions. Range checking parameters is useful for debugging. After youvalidate your program, you can set this attribute to VI_FALSE to disable range checkingand maximize performance.

Use the niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function to override thedefault value.

Default Value: VI_TRUE.

# High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RECORD_COERCIONS

Specifies whether the IVI engine records the value coercions it makes for ViInt32 andViReal64 attributes. Call the niDCPower_GetNextCoercionRecord function to read anddelete the earliest coercion record from the list.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RECORD_COERCIONS

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050006</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Default Value: The default value is VI_FALSE. Use theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function to override this value.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SIMULATE

Specifies whether to simulate NI-DCPower I/O operations. VI_TRUE specifies thatoperation is simulated.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SIMULATE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1050005</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>N/A</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Do not simulate NI-DCPower I/O operations.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true (1)</td><td>Simulate NI-DCPower I/O operations.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value: VI_FALSE

# High-Level Functions:

niDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels

# LCR

AC Stimulus

# Groups

• Advanced


Group members


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AUTOMATIC_LEVEL_CONTROL</td><td>Specifies whether the channel actively attempts to maintain a constant test voltage or current across the DUT for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_AMPLITUDE</td><td>Specifies the amplitude, in A RMS, of the AC current test signal applied to the DUT for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_FREQ</td><td>Specifies the frequency of the AC test signal applied to the DUT for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_STIMULUS_FUNCTION</td><td>Specifies the type of test signal to apply to the DUT for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_AMPLITUDE</td><td>Specifies the amplitude, in V RMS, of the AC voltage test signal applied to the DUT for LCR measurements.</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

Advanced

# Groups

None


Group members


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AC_DITHER_ENABLED</td><td>Specifies whether dithering is enabled during LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_RANGE</td><td>Specifies the current range, in amps RMS, that defines the values to which you can set the LCR current amplitude (NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_AMPLITUDE) for the specified channel(s).</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SOURCE_APERTURE_TIME</td><td>Specifies the LCR source aperture time, in seconds, for a channel in LCR mode.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_RANGE</td><td>Specifies the voltage range, in volts RMS, that defines the values to which you can set the LCR voltage amplitude (NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_AMPLITUDE) for the specified channel(s).</td></tr></table>

# Attachments

None

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AC_DITHER_ENABLED

Specifies whether dithering is enabled during LCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AC_DITHER_ENABLED

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150348</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Dithering adds out-of-band noise to improve measurements of small voltage andcurrent signals.




# Note

Hardware is only warranted to meet its accuracy specs with dither enabled.You can disable dither if the added noise interferes with your device-under-test.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Dithering is not applied to LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true (1)</td><td>Dithering is applied to LCR measurements.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_RANGE

Specifies the current range, in amps RMS, that defines the values to which you can setthe LCR current amplitude (NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_AMPLITUDE) for thespecified channel(s).

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_RANGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150267</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

For valid ranges, refer to the specifications for your instrument.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SOURCE_APERTURE_TI

Specifies the LCR source aperture time, in seconds, for a channel in LCR mode.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SOURCE_APERTURE_TIME

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150349</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Use this attribute to adjust the aperture time for the LCR control loop. In systems thatuse multiple tones that are equally spaced, setting this attribute to 1/(Tone Spacing inHz) can reduce the impact of external interference and still maintain an integernumber of cycles for all multi-tones. Increasing LCR source aperture time can increasethe required settling time.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Valid Values:

5.0e-6 s to 100.0e-3 s

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_RANGE GE_RANGE

Specifies the voltage range, in volts RMS, that defines the values to which you can setthe LCR voltage amplitude (NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_AMPLITUDE) for thespecified channel(s).

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_VOLTAGE_RANGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150265</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

For valid ranges, refer to the specifications for your instrument.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AUTOMATIC_LEVEL_C TIC_LEVEL_CONTROL

Specifies whether the channel actively attempts to maintain a constant test voltage orcurrent across the DUT for LCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_AUTOMATIC_LEVEL_CONTROL

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150290</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The use of voltage or current depends on the test signal you configure with theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_STIMULUS_FUNCTION attribute.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_OFF</td><td>0 (0x0)</td><td>Automatic level control is off.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON</td><td>1 (0x1)</td><td>Automatic level control is on.</td></tr></table>




# Note

Because the PXIe-4190 output impedance is a function of the impedancerange, selecting this option may result in an actual AC stimulus amplitude atthe load that is different from the value you specify. Impedancemeasurements take this difference into account and remain accurate.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_AMPLITUDE

Specifies the amplitude, in A RMS, of the AC current test signal applied to the DUT forLCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_CURRENT_AMPLITUDE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150212</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

This attribute applies when the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_STIMULUS_FUNCTIONattribute is set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_AC_CURRENT.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Valid Values:

7.08e-9 A RMS to 0.707 A RMS

Instrument specifications affect the valid values you can program. Refer to thespecifications for your instrument for more information.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_FREQUENCY

40 Hz to 2 MHz

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_ACTUAL_LOAD_REACTANCE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150270</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

This attribute applies when

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_OPEN_SHORT_LOAD_COMPENSATION_DATA_SOURCE is set

to NIDCPOWER_VAL_AS_DEFINED.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_LOAD_COMPENSA OMPENSATIONTION

Specifies whether to apply load LCR compensation data to LCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_LOAD_COMPENSATION_ENABLED

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150222</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Both the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_OPEN_COMPENSATION_ENABLED andNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SHORT_COMPENSATION_ENABLED attributes must be set toVI_TRUE in order to set this attribute to VI_TRUE.

Use the

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_OPEN_SHORT_LOAD_COMPENSATION_DATA_SOURCE

attribute to define where the load compensation data that is applied to LCR

measurements comes from.

Load compensation data are applied only for those specific frequencies youdefine with niDCPower_PerformLCRLoadCompensation; load compensationis not interpolated from the specific frequencies you define and applied toother frequencies.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Load LCR compensation data are applied.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true (1)</td><td>Load LCR compensation data are not applied.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150223</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

Attributes by Device for information about supported instruments.


Defined Values:


<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ONBOARD_STORAGE</td><td>1074(0x432)</td><td>Uses previously generated LCR compensation data. Only the most recently performed compensation data for each LCR compensation type (open, short, and load) is stored.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AS_defined</td><td>1075(0x433)</td><td>Uses the LCR compensation data represented by the relevant LCR compensation attributes as generated by niDCPower_PerformLCROpenCompensation, niDCPower_PerformLCRShortCompensation, and niDCPower_PerformLCRLoadCompensation. Use this option to manage multiple sets of LCR compensation data. This option applies compensation data from the following attributes:NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_OPEN_CONDUCTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_OPEN SUSCEPTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR SHORT_RESISTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR SHORT_REACTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_MEASURED_LOAD_RESISTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_MEASURED_LOAD_REACTANCE,NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_ACTUAL_LOAD_RESISTANCE,orNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_ACTUAL_LOAD_REACTANCE</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AS_CONFIGURED</td><td>1146(0x47a)</td><td>Uses the LCR compensation data supplied to niDCPower_ConfigureLCRCompensation. Use this option to manage multiple sets of LCR compensation data.</td></tr></table>


Default Value:


Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SHORCR_SHORT_CUSTOM_CABLE_COM_CABLE_COMPENSAOMPENSATION_ENABLEDTION_ENABLED

Defines how to apply short custom cable compensation in LCR mode when

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CABLE_LENGTH is set to

NIDCPOWER_VAL_CUSTOM_ONBOARD_STORAGE or

NIDCPOWER_VAL_CUSTOM_AS_CONFIGURED.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SHORT_CUSTOM_CABLE_COMPENSATION_ENABLED

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150299</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

LCR custom cable compensation uses compensation data for both an open and shortconfiguration.

• For open custom cable compensation, you must supply your own data from a callto niDCPower_PerformLCROpenCustomCableCompensation.

• For short custom cable compensation, you can supply your own data from a call toniDCPower_PerformLCRShortCustomCableCompensation or NI-DCPower canapply a default set of short compensation data.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE(0)</td><td>Uses default short compensation data.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true(1)</td><td>Uses short custom cable compensation data generated by niDCPower.PerformLCRShortCustomCableCompensation.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_DC_BIAS_A CR_DC_BIAS_AUTOMATIC_LEVEL_C TIC_LEVEL_CONTROL

Specifies whether the channel actively maintains a constant DC bias voltage or currentacross the DUT for LCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_DC_BIAS_AUTOMATIC_LEVEL_CONTROL

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150291</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

To use this attribute, you must configure a DC bias by:

• Selecting an NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_DC_BIAS_SOURCE

• Depending on the DC bias source you choose, setting either the




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_OFF</td><td>0 (0x0)</td><td>Automatic level control is off.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON</td><td>1 (0x1)</td><td>Automatic level control is on.</td></tr></table>




# Note

Because the PXIe-4190 output impedance is a function of the impedancerange, selecting this option may result in an actual DC bias value at the loadthat is different from the value you specify. Impedance measurements takethis difference into account and remain accurate.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

Specifies the DC bias current level, in amperes, when the

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_DC_BIAS_SOURCE is set to

NIDCPOWER_VAL_DC_BIAS_CURRENT.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_DC_BIAS_CURRENT_LEVEL

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Valid Values:

-0.1 A to 0.1 A

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE_SO

Specifies how the impedance range for LCR measurements is determined.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE_SOURCE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150321</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_AUTO_RANGE overrides anyimpedance range determined by this attribute.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE</td><td>1142(0x476)</td><td>Uses the impedance range you specify with theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGEattribute.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_LCR_LOAD_CONFIGURATION</td><td>1143(0x477)</td><td>Computes the impedance range to selectbased on the values you supply to theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_LOADResistance,</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_LOAD_INDUCTANCE, and NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_LOAD_CAPACITANCE attributes. NI-DCPower uses a series model of load resistance, load inductance, and load capacitance to compute the impedance range.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

(0 farads, +inf farads)

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDCR_IMPEDANCE_AUTO_RANGEO_RANGE

Defines whether an instrument in LCR mode automatically selects the best impedancerange for each given LCR measurement.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_AUTO_RANGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150216</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Impedance autoranging may be enabled only when both:

• The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_MODE attribute is set to

• NIDCPOWER_VAL_SINGLE_POINT

# The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHEN attribute is set to a value other thanNIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER

You can read NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE back after a measurementto determine the actual range used.

When enabled, impedance autoranging overrides impedance range settings youconfigure manually with any other attributes.




# Note

When using a load with unknown impedance, you can set this attribute toNIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTO_RANGE_ON to determine the correct impedancerange for the load. When you know the load impedance, you can achievefaster performance by setting this attribute toNIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTO_RANGE_OFF and settingNIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE_SOURCE toNIDCPOWER_VAL_LCR_LOAD_CONFIGURATION.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTO_RANGE_OFF</td><td>1068(0x42c)</td><td>Disables automatic selection of the impedance range. Channel(s) use the impedance range you specify with the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_IMPEDANCE_RANGE</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td>attribute.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTO_RANGE_ON</td><td>1070(0x42e)</td><td>Channel(s) automatically select the optimal NIDCPOWER_ATTR LCR IMPEDANCE RANGE for the measured signal.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_MEASUREMENT_TIME

Selects a general aperture time profile for LCR measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_MEASUREMENT_TIME

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150218</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The actual duration of each profile depends on the frequency of the LCR test signal.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_MEASUREMENT_TIME SHORT</td><td>1071(0x42f)</td><td>Uses a short aperture time for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_MEASUREMENT_TIME_MEDIUM</td><td>1072(0x430)</td><td>Uses a medium aperture time for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_MEASUREMENT_TIME LONG</td><td>1073(0x431)</td><td>Uses a long aperture time for LCR measurements.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_MEASUREMENT_TIMEcustom</td><td>1117(0x45d)</td><td>Uses a custom aperture time for LCR measurement specified by the NIDCPOWER_ATTR LCR Custom MEASUREMENT attribute.</td></tr></table>

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by instrument.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SOURCE_DELCR_SOURCE_DELAY_MODE

For instruments in LCR mode, determines whether NI-DCPower automaticallycalculates and applies the source delay or applies a source delay you set manually.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_LCR_SOURCE_DELAY_MODE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150315</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

You can return the source delay duration for either option by readingNIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_DELAY.




# Note

When you use this attribute to manually set the source delay, it is possible toset source delays short enough to unbalance the bridge and affectmeasurement accuracy. LCR measurement functions report whether thebridge is unbalanced.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all instruments. Refer to SupportedAttributes by Device for information about supported instruments.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_LCR_SOURCE_DELAY_MODE_AUTOMATIC</td><td>1144(0x478)</td><td>NI-DCPower automatically applies source delay of sufficient duration to account for settling time.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_LCR_SOURCE_DELAY_MODE_manual</td><td>1145(0x479)</td><td>NI-DCPower applies the source delay that you set manually with NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_DELAY.You can use this option to set a shorter delay to reduce measurement time at the possible expense of measurement accuracy.</td></tr></table>

Default Value: Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE

Specifies whether the hardware automatically selects the best range to measure thesignal. Note the highest range the algorithm uses is dependent on the correspondinglimit range property. The algorithm the hardware uses can be controlled using theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_APERTURE_TIME_MODE attribute.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150244</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This property is not supported by all devices. Refer to the SupportedAttributes by Device for information about devices that support this attribute.




# Note

Autoranging begins at instrument startup and remains active until theinstrument is reconfigured or reset.

Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_OFF</td><td>0(0x0)</td><td>The hardware does not select the range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON</td><td>1(0x1)</td><td>The hardware selects the range based on the compliance range and autorange attributes.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_APERORANGE_APERTURE_TIME_MODETURE_TIME_MODE

Specifies whether the aperture time used for the measurement autorange algorithm isdetermined automatically or customized using the

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_APERTURE_TIME attribute.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_APERTURE_TIME_MODE

# Remarks




Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO</td><td>1110(0x456)</td><td>NI-DCPower optimizes the aperture time for the autoraia based on the module range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIMECustom</td><td>1111(0x457)</td><td>The user specifies a minimum aperture time for the algo the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE MINIMUM APERTURE attribute and the corresponding NIDCPOWER_ATTR AUTORANGE MINIMUM APERTURE attribute.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_BEHA ORANGE_BEHAVIOR

Specifies the algorithm the hardware uses for measurement autoranging.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_BEHAVIOR

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>1150245</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_RANGE_UP_TO_LIMIT THEN_DOWN</td><td>1107(0x453)</td><td>Range up to maximum, then down-Go to limit range then range down as needed until measured value is within thresholds.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_RANGE_UP</td><td>1108(0x454)</td><td>Range up-Go up one range when the upper threshold is reached.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_RANGE_UP_AND_DOWN</td><td>1109(0x455)</td><td>Range up and down-Go up or down one range when the upper/ lower threshold is reached.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MAORANGE_MAXIMUM_DELXIMUM_DELAY_AFTER_RANGE_CHANGEY_AFTER_RANGE_CHANGE

Balances between settling time and maximum measurement time by specifying themaximum time delay between when a range change occurs and when measurementsresume.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MAXIMUM_DELAY_AFTER_RANGE_CHANGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150322</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Valid Values: The minimum and maximum values of this attribute are hardware-dependent.

• PXIe-4135/4136/4137: 0 to 9 seconds

• PXIe-4138/4139: 0 to 9 seconds

• PXIe-4147: 0 to 9 seconds

• PXIe-4162/4163: 0 to 0.1 seconds

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_APERORANGE_MINIMUM_APERTURE_TIMETURE_TIME

Specifies the measurement autorange aperture time used for the measurementautorange algorithm. The aperture time is specified in the units set by theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_APERTURE_TIME_UNITS attribute. Thisvalue will typically be smaller than the aperture time used for measurements.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_APERTURE_TIME

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

initialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_CORANGE_MINIMUM_CURRENT_RANGEURRENT_RANGE

Limiting the lowest range used during autoranging can improve the speed of theautoranging algorithm and minimize frequent and unpredictable range changes fornoisy signals. The maximum range used is the range that includes the value specifiedin the compliance limit attribute, NIDCPOWER_ATTR_VOLTAGE_LIMIT_RANGE attributeor NIDCPOWER_ATTR_CURRENT_LIMIT_RANGE attribute, depending on the selectedNIDCPOWER_ATTR_OUTPUT_FUNCTION.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_MINIMUM_CURRENT_RANGE

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_THRESHOLD_MODE

Specifies thresholds used during autoranging to determine when range changingoccurs.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE_THRESHOLD_MODE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150257</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_THRESH_MODE_NORMAL</td><td>1112(0x458)</td><td>Thresholds are selected based on a balance between accuracy and hysteresis.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_THRESH_MODE_FAST_STEP</td><td>1113(0x459)</td><td>Optimized for faster changes in the measured signal. Thresholds are configured to be a smaller percentage of the range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_THRESH_MODE_HIGH_HYSTERESIS</td><td>1114(0x45a)</td><td>Optimized for noisy signals to minimize frequent and unpredictable range changes. Thresholds are configured to be a larger percentage of the range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_THRESH_MODEMedium_HYSTERESIS</td><td>1115(0x45b)</td><td>Optimized for faster changes in the measured signal. Thresholds are configured to be a smaller percentage of the range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_THRESH_MODE_HOLD</td><td>1116(0x45c)</td><td>Attempt to maintain the active range. Thresholds will favor the active range.</td></tr></table>

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_BUFFER_SIZE

Specifies the number of samples that the active channel measurement buffer canhold.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_BUFFER_SIZE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150077</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

The default value is the maximum number of samples that a device is capable of

recording in one second.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Valid Range: For the PXIe-4051, PXIe-4147 and PXIe-4150/4151, 170 to 18000110. Forthe PXIe-4162/4163, 256 to 1000192. For the PXIe-4190, 102 to 6000048. For thePXIe-4112, PXIe-4113, and PXIe-4154, 1000 to 178956970. For all other supportedinstruments, 1000 to 268435455.

Default Value: Varies by device. Refer to the Supported Attributes by Device topic inthe NI DC Power Supplies and SMUs Help for more information about defaultvalues.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHEN

Specifies when the measure unit should acquire measurements. Unless this attributeis configured to NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER, theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_TRIGGER_TYPE attribute is ignored.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHEN

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150057</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

NI-DCPower uses the terms "source" and "output". However, while sinkingwith electronic loads and SMUs these correspond to "sinking" and "input",respectively.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATICALLY_AFTER_SOURCE_COMPLETE</td><td>1025(0x401)</td><td>Acquires a measurementafter each Source Completeevent.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_DEMAND</td><td>1026</td><td>Acquires a measurement</td></tr><tr><td></td><td>(0x402)</td><td>when the niDCPower_Measure function or niDCPower_MeasureMultiple function is called.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER</td><td>1027 (0x403)</td><td>Acquires a measurement when a Measure trigger is received.</td></tr></table>

Default Value: If the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_MODE attribute is set toNIDCPOWER_VAL_SINGLE_POINT, the default value isNIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_DEMAND. This value supports only the niDCPower_Measurefunction and niDCPower_MeasureMultiple function. If theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_MODE attribute is set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_SEQUENCE,the default value is NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATICALLY_AFTER_SOURCE_COMPLETE.This value supports only the niDCPower_FetchMultiple function.

# Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RESET_ATTR_RESET_AVERAGE_BEFORE_MEASUREMENTORE_MEASUREMENT

Specifies whether the measurement returned from any measurement call starts with anew measurement call (VI_TRUE) or returns a measurement that has already begun orcompleted(VI_FALSE).

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_RESET_AVERAGE_BEFORE_MEASUREMENT

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150006</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

When you set the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SAMPLES_TO_AVERAGE attribute in the Runningstate, the channel measurements might move out of synchronization. While NI-DCPower automatically synchronizes measurements upon the initialization of asession, you can force a synchronization in the running state before you run theniDCPower_MeasureMultiple function. To force a synchronization in the running state,set this attribute to VI_TRUE, and then run the niDCPower_MeasureMultiple function,specifying all channels in the channel name parameter. You can set theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_RESET_AVERAGE_BEFORE_MEASUREMENT attribute to VI_FALSEafter the niDCPower_MeasureMultiple function completes.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Do not reset the average before measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>VI:true (1)</td><td>Reset the average before measurement.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# Related Topics:

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME

Specifies the measurement aperture time for the channel configuration. Aperture timeis specified in the units set by the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME_UNITSattribute.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150058</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# High-Level Functions:

• niDCPower_ConfigureApertureTime

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APER TTR_APERTURE_TIME_A TURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE

Automatically optimizes the measurement aperture time according to the actualcurrent range when measurement autorange is enabled. Optimization accounts forpower line frequency when the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME_UNITS attributeis set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_POWER_LINE_CYCLES.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150314</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

This attribute is applicable only if the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_OUTPUT_FUNCTIONattribute is set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_DC_VOLTAGE and theNIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE attribute is set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON.




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE_OFF</td><td>1135(0x46f)</td><td>Disables automatic aperture time scaling. The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME attribute specifies the aperture time for all ranges.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE SHORT</td><td>1136(0x470)</td><td>Prioritizes measurement speed over measurement accuracy by quickly scaling down aperture time in larger current ranges. The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME attribute specifies the aperture time for the minimum range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE_NORMAL</td><td>1137(0x471)</td><td>Balances measurement accuracy and speed by scaling down aperture time in larger current ranges. The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME attribute specifies the aperture time for the minimum range.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_APERTURE_TIME_AUTO_MODE_long</td><td>1138(0x472)</td><td>Prioritizes accuracy while still decreasing measurement time by slowly scaling down aperture time in larger current ranges. The NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME attribute specifies the aperture time for the minimum range.</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTORANGE

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTO_ZERO

Specifies the auto-zero method to use on the device.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_AUTO_ZERO

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150055</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_OFF</td><td>0 (0x0)</td><td>Disables auto-zero.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ONCE</td><td>1024(0x400)</td><td>Makes zero conversions following the first measurement after initiating the channel(s). Channels use these zero conversions for the preceding measurement and future measurements until they are reinitiated.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON</td><td>1 (0x1)</td><td>Makes zero conversions for every measurement.</td></tr></table>

• niDCPower_ConfigureAutoZero

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_REC TTR_MEASURE_RECORD_DEL ORD_DELTA_TIME

Queries the amount of time, in seconds, between the start of two consecutivemeasurements in a measure record. Only query this attribute after the desiredmeasurement settings are committed.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_RECORD_DELTA_TIME

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150065</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read-Only</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

This attribute is not available when Auto Zero is configured to Once becausethe amount of time between the first two measurements and the rest woulddiffer.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_RECORD_LENGTH

Specifies how many measurements compose a measure record. When this attribute isset to a value greater than 1, the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHEN attribute mustbe set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATICALLY_AFTER_SOURCE_COMPLETE orNIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_RECORD_LENGTH

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150063</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Valid Values: 1 to 16,777,216

Default Value: 1




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

Related Topics:

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_RECORD_LENGTH_IS_FINITE

Specifies whether to take continuous measurements. Call theniDCPower_AbortWithChannels function to stop continuous measurements. When thisattribute is set to VI_FALSE and the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_MODE attribute is setto NIDCPOWER_VAL_SINGLE_POINT, the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHENattribute must be set toNIDCPOWER_VAL_AUTOMATICALLY_AFTER_SOURCE_COMPLETE orNIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER. When this attribute is set to VI_FALSE andthe NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SOURCE_MODE attribute is set toNIDCPOWER_VAL_SEQUENCE, the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_WHEN attributemust be set to NIDCPOWER_VAL_ON_MEASURE_TRIGGER.

Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_MEASURE_RECORD_LENGTH_IS_FINITE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150064</td><td>ViBoolean</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.




# Note

To specify a channel name when accessing this attribute, you must firstinitialize the session using theniDCPower_InitializeWithIndependentChannels function.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>VI_FALSE (0)</td><td>Allows continuous measurement.</td></tr><tr><td>VI_true (1)</td><td>Does not allow continuous measurement.</td></tr></table>

Default Value: VI_TRUE

Related Topics:

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_LINE_FREQUENCY

Specifies the power line frequency for specified channel(s). NI-DCPower uses this valueto select a timebase for setting the NIDCPOWER_ATTR_APERTURE_TIME attribute inpower line cycles (PLCs).

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_POWER_LINE_FREQUENCY

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150020</td><td>ViReal64</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

This attribute is not supported by all devices. Refer to Supported Attributesby Device for information about supported devices.

Refer to the measurement configuration and timing topics and DC noise rejectiontopics of supported devices for more information about how to configure yourmeasurements.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_50_HERTZ</td><td>50.0</td><td>Specifies a power line frequency of 50 Hz.</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_60_HERTZ</td><td>60.0</td><td>Specifies a power line frequency of 60 Hz.</td></tr></table>

Default Value:NIDCPOWER_VAL_60_HERTZ

High-Level Functions:

• niDCPower_ConfigurePowerLineFrequency

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SAMPLES_T TTR_SAMPLES_TO_AVERAGE

Specifies the number of samples to average when you take a measurement.

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SAMPLES_TO_AVERAGE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150003</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks

Increasing the number of samples to average decreases measurement noise butincreases the time required to take a measurement.

<table><tr><td>Device</td><td>Range of Samples to Average</td></tr><tr><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td>NI PXI-4110 and NI PXI-4130</td><td>1 to 511 samples</td></tr><tr><td>NI PXI-4132</td><td>1 to 127 samples</td></tr><tr><td>NI PXIe-4154</td><td>1 to 65,535 samples</td></tr><tr><td>All other supported instruments</td><td>1</td></tr></table>

# Default Value:

Refer to Supported Attributes by Device for the default value by device.

# NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SENSE

Selects either local or remote sensing of the output voltage for the specifiedchannel(s).

# Syntax

NIDCPOWER_ATTR_SENSE

<table><tr><td>Numeric Value</td><td>Data Type</td><td>Access</td><td>Applies To</td></tr><tr><td>1150013</td><td>VInt32</td><td>Read/Write</td><td>Channels</td></tr></table>

# Remarks




# Note

NI-DCPower uses the terms "source" and "output". However, while sinkingwith electronic loads and SMUs these correspond to "sinking" and "input",respectively.

# Defined Values:

<table><tr><td>Name</td><td>Value</td><td>Description</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VAL_LOCAL</td><td>1008 (0x3f0)</td><td>Local sensing</td></tr><tr><td>NIDCPOWER_VALREMOTE</td><td>1009 (0x3f1)</td><td>Remote sensing</td></tr></table>

Default Value: The default value is NIDCPOWER_VAL_LOCAL if the device supportslocal sense. Otherwise, the default and only supported value isNIDCPOWER_VAL_REMOTE.

# High-Level Functions:

• niDCPower_ConfigureSense
